verses.text: In the twenty-fifth year of exile, God brings Ezekiel to a high mountain, where a man with a measuring reed bids him 'mark well all that I shall shew thee.' A vision of the temple. God renews hope by showing His house restored.

In the twenty-fifth year of exile, God brings Ezekiel to a high mountain, where a man with a measuring reed bids him 'mark well all that I shall shew thee.' A vision of the temple. God renews hope by showing His house restored.

In the twenty-fifth year of exile, God brings Ezekiel to a high mountain, where a man with a measuring reed bids him 'mark well all that I shall shew thee.' A vision of the temple. God renews hope by showing His house restored.

In the twenty-fifth year of exile, God brings Ezekiel to a high mountain, where a man with a measuring reed bids him 'mark well all that I shall shew thee.' A vision of the temple. God renews hope by showing His house restored.
A wall surrounds the house; the man measures the wall and the gate's threshold and chambers with the reed. Careful measure. God's dwelling is built to exact, holy proportion.
A wall surrounds the house; the man measures the wall and the gate's threshold and chambers with the reed. Careful measure. God's dwelling is built to exact, holy proportion.
A wall surrounds the house; the man measures the wall and the gate's threshold and chambers with the reed. Careful measure. God's dwelling is built to exact, holy proportion.
He measures the gate's porch and its little chambers, three on each side, all 'of one measure.' Uniform order. Nothing in God's house is haphazard or careless.
He measures the gate's porch and its little chambers, three on each side, all 'of one measure.' Uniform order. Nothing in God's house is haphazard or careless.
He measures the gate's porch and its little chambers, three on each side, all 'of one measure.' Uniform order. Nothing in God's house is haphazard or careless.
He measures the entry's breadth and the gate from roof to roof. Precise dimensions. The Spirit records every detail of the place of worship.
He measures the entry's breadth and the gate from roof to roof. Precise dimensions. The Spirit records every detail of the place of worship.
He measures the entry's breadth and the gate from roof to roof. Precise dimensions. The Spirit records every detail of the place of worship.
Posts of sixty cubits, narrow windows to the chambers, and palm trees on the posts. Beauty and light. God's house joins strength with graceful adornment.
Posts of sixty cubits, narrow windows to the chambers, and palm trees on the posts. Beauty and light. God's house joins strength with graceful adornment.
Posts of sixty cubits, narrow windows to the chambers, and palm trees on the posts. Beauty and light. God's house joins strength with graceful adornment.
He is brought to the outer court, with thirty chambers upon a pavement. Room for the people. God's house has place for all who come to worship.
He is brought to the outer court, with thirty chambers upon a pavement. Room for the people. God's house has place for all who come to worship.
He is brought to the outer court, with thirty chambers upon a pavement. Room for the people. God's house has place for all who come to worship.
The north gate is measured, with chambers, posts, and arches, like the first. Consistent pattern. Every entrance to God is ordered alike.
The north gate is measured, with chambers, posts, and arches, like the first. Consistent pattern. Every entrance to God is ordered alike.
The north gate is measured, with chambers, posts, and arches, like the first. Consistent pattern. Every entrance to God is ordered alike.
The north gate is measured, with chambers, posts, and arches, like the first. Consistent pattern. Every entrance to God is ordered alike.
The south gate likewise, with seven steps up to it. Ascent to worship. To draw near to God is to rise, step by step, toward Him.
The south gate likewise, with seven steps up to it. Ascent to worship. To draw near to God is to rise, step by step, toward Him.
The south gate likewise, with seven steps up to it. Ascent to worship. To draw near to God is to rise, step by step, toward Him.
The south gate likewise, with seven steps up to it. Ascent to worship. To draw near to God is to rise, step by step, toward Him.
He is brought to the inner court by the south gate, its arches toward the outer court, with eight steps. Deeper access. The nearer to God's house, the higher the climb.
He is brought to the inner court by the south gate, its arches toward the outer court, with eight steps. Deeper access. The nearer to God's house, the higher the climb.
He is brought to the inner court by the south gate, its arches toward the outer court, with eight steps. Deeper access. The nearer to God's house, the higher the climb.
He is brought to the inner court by the south gate, its arches toward the outer court, with eight steps. Deeper access. The nearer to God's house, the higher the climb.
The eastern inner gate is measured the same, with its arches and eight steps. Symmetry maintained. God's order holds on every side of His house.
The eastern inner gate is measured the same, with its arches and eight steps. Symmetry maintained. God's order holds on every side of His house.
The eastern inner gate is measured the same, with its arches and eight steps. Symmetry maintained. God's order holds on every side of His house.
The north inner gate is measured alike, with palm trees and eight steps. Unbroken pattern. The whole sanctuary speaks of one ordered design.
The north inner gate is measured alike, with palm trees and eight steps. Unbroken pattern. The whole sanctuary speaks of one ordered design.
The north inner gate is measured alike, with palm trees and eight steps. Unbroken pattern. The whole sanctuary speaks of one ordered design.
By the gate are chambers where the burnt offering is washed, with tables for the sacrifices. Provision for worship. God appoints the place and means of approach by sacrifice.
By the gate are chambers where the burnt offering is washed, with tables for the sacrifices. Provision for worship. God appoints the place and means of approach by sacrifice.
By the gate are chambers where the burnt offering is washed, with tables for the sacrifices. Provision for worship. God appoints the place and means of approach by sacrifice.
Eight tables stand for slaying the sacrifices, with hooks fastened within. Ready service. Everything is prepared for the offerings God's worship requires.
Eight tables stand for slaying the sacrifices, with hooks fastened within. Ready service. Everything is prepared for the offerings God's worship requires.
Eight tables stand for slaying the sacrifices, with hooks fastened within. Ready service. Everything is prepared for the offerings God's worship requires.
Chambers are set for the singers and for the priests who keep the charge of the house and altar. Order of ministry. Praise and priesthood each have their appointed place.
Chambers are set for the singers and for the priests who keep the charge of the house and altar. Order of ministry. Praise and priesthood each have their appointed place.
Chambers are set for the singers and for the priests who keep the charge of the house and altar. Order of ministry. Praise and priesthood each have their appointed place.
He measures the inner court foursquare, the altar before the house, and the porch with its pillars and steps. The altar central. At the heart of God's house stands the place of sacrifice.
He measures the inner court foursquare, the altar before the house, and the porch with its pillars and steps. The altar central. At the heart of God's house stands the place of sacrifice.
He measures the inner court foursquare, the altar before the house, and the porch with its pillars and steps. The altar central. At the heart of God's house stands the place of sacrifice.
